title: Turkish Potato Kisir
description: A traditional Turkish-inspired potato dish with a mustardy, glassy-jelly texture. Kisir is a Turkish salad, but with the addition of potato starch, it takes on a unique, dense form.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together potato starch, vinegar, and 1/4 cup water to make a smooth paste.
Add the confit tomatoes, red onion, garlic, thyme, salt, and black pepper to the bowl. Stir until well combined.
Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the potato starch mixture and stir constantly for 2-3 minutes, or until it thickens and turns a deep amber color.
Remove the skillet from heat and let it cool slightly. Stir in the tomato mixture and let it cool to room temperature.
Once cooled, refrigerate the mixture for at least 2 hours, or until it has firmed up and developed a glassy texture.
Serve the Turkish potato kisir chilled, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
